label: Physics
description: A simple 2D rigid-body physics engine. Each top-level shape in the main geometry input becomes a dynamic body (a convex hull of its real outline) that falls under gravity, tumbles, lands on the page floor, and collides with the other bodies. Gravity is given in m/s² (Earth ≈ 9.81). The Mass slider scales how heavy the falling pieces are - heavier pieces resist being pushed and settle more firmly. The Sleep Speed slider (px/s) sets the speed under which a resting piece is treated as still and frozen: raise it if pieces still jitter after landing (they lock down sooner), lower it for longer-lived, more precise motion. The optional Collision input accepts kinematic collider geometry: it is immovable, but its Collider Mass scales how hard it shoves the dynamic bodies; if you animate the collider it sweeps from its rest pose to its current animated pose across the bake, imparting momentum to whatever it hits (like a paddle). Turn on Contain Inside Collider to invert the collision so the dynamic bodies are kept INSIDE the collider's cavity instead of bouncing off its outside - use this to drop a ball into a bowl, ring, or frame and have it stay inside. The whole simulation is baked from frame 0 up to the current timeline frame, so scrubbing and export stay deterministic. Enable Animation and scrub/play the timeline to watch pieces fall and collide.
